post.summary: Creates a temporary job for this deployment, executes it, and immediately discards the job created by the execution.
post.description:
Creates a temporary job for this deployment, executes it with the provided input, and immediately discards the job created by the execution. The call will wait until execution has finished. If the workflow contains a report and the optional format query parameter is passed then the result if this operation will be the rendered report.
In contrast to `/execution` the request body will be passed to the job unaltered and can be consumed by a _Container Input (Raw HTTP)_ node. Likewise the output must be produced by a _Container Output (Raw HTTP)_ node and will be the  content of the response body - except if the workflow contains a report, see above.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: An optional timeout in milliseconds. In case the job is not loaded and fully executed within the provided timeout a respective response is returned. In case no timeout is provided the default timeout of the execution context is used.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.description: Set to true if the job should be reset prior to execution.

parameters.name: keepJob
parameters.description: Set to true if the job should be kept after execution and not get discarded immediately.

schema.description: Arbitrary input that is passed to the _Container Input (Raw HTTP)_ node.
Arbitrary output that is created by the _Container Input (Raw HTTP)_ node in the workflow or a report.

403.description: The calling user does not have the rights to execute the deployment.

404.description: The provided deployment does not exist.

422.description: The provided input is not valid.

504.description: The job did not finish within time.

get.summary: Creates a temporary job for this deployment, executes it, and immediately discards the job created by the execution.
get.description:
Creates a tempory job for this deployment, executes it with the provided input, and immediately discards the job created by the execution. The call will wait until execution has finished. If the workflow contains a report and the optional format query parameter is passed then the result if this operation will be the rendered report.
Since this is a `GET` request you cannot pass any data in the request body. The output must be produced by a _Container Output (Raw HTTP)_ node and will be the content of the response body - except if the workflow contains a report, see above.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: An optional timeout in milliseconds. In case the job is not loaded and fully executed within the provided timeout a respective response is returned. In case no timeout is provided the default timeout of the execution context is used.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.description: Set to true if the job should be reset prior to execution.

parameters.name: keepJob
parameters.description: Set to true if the job should be kept after execution and not get discarded immediately.

schema.description: Arbitrary output that is created by the _Container Input (Raw HTTP)_ node in the workflow or a report.

403.description: The calling user does not have the rights to execute the deployment.

404.description: The provided deployment does not exist.

422.description: The provided input is not valid.

504.description: The job did not finish within time.

get.summary: Returns all jobs of the specific deployment.
get.description: The returned list will only contain jobs that the current user can read. Only the administrator can see all jobs.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Header used to check if a change occurred since the last request.

304.description: Nothing changed since the last request.

403.description: The calling user does not have the rights for this endpoint.

422.description: The provided workflow filter is invalid.

post.summary: Creates a new job for the provided deployment.
post.description: Creates a new job for the provided deployment.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: An optional load timeout in milliseconds. In case the job is not loaded within the provided timeout a respective response is returned. In case no timeout is provided the default timeout of the execution context is used.

parameters.name: asyncLoading
parameters.description: An optional flag to load the job asynchronously, i.e. the service returns directly after sending a load message to the message broker. The state of a job that has not been loaded yet is LOADING.

403.description: The calling user does not have the rights to execute the workflow.

504.description: The job was not loaded within time.

post.summary: Executes a job of this deployment.
post.description: This call combines loading, executing, and deleting a job in one call. If no data is sent the job is executed with the current input parameters (if there are any), otherwise the input parameters are set according to the JSON map that is sent in the request body. By adding 'edge-compatible' to the 'Prefer' header of the request you can ask the server to return only the job's output values instead of all metadata.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.description: If the job should be reset before execution.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The report format that should be returned in case the job generates one.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: An optional timeout in milliseconds for setting input parameters. In case the input parameters are not set within the provided timeout a respective response is returned. In case no timeout is provided the default load timeout of the deployment is used.

200.description: The job status or the report.

403.description: The calling user does not have the rights to execute the job.

422.description: The provided input is not valid.

504.description: The job did not finish within time.

get.summary: Executes a job of this deployment.
get.description: This call combines loading, executing, and deleting a job in one call. This call behaves similarly to the POST method above but allows you to provide input parameters as query parameters.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.description: If the job should be reset before execution.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The report format that should be returned in case the job generates one.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: An optional timeout in milliseconds for setting input parameters. In case the input parameters are not set within the provided timeout a respective response is returned. In case no timeout is provided the default load timeout of the deployment is used.

200.description: The job status or the report.

403.description: The calling user does not have the rights to execute the job.

422.description: The provided input is not valid.

504.description: The job did not finish within time.

Control.description: A hypermedia element associated with this resource.

Error.description: A potential error attached to this Mason object.

Namespace.description: A (possibly empty) map with the namespaces for this object.

@controls.description: A (possibly empty) map with all controls for this object.

@namespaces.description: A (possibly empty) map with the namespaces for this object.

@controls.description: A (possibly empty) map with all controls for this object.

@namespaces.description: A (possibly empty) map with the namespaces for this object.

schedulerId.description: The ID of the scheduled job if triggered by one.

parentId.description: The parent id if this job has been triggered due to an action or failure of another job.

executorName.description: The name of the executor the job is loaded in. Not present if the job is swapped.

executorIPs.description: The executor's IP adresses the job is loaded in. Not present if the job is swapped.

executorId.description: The id of the executor the job is loaded in. Not present if the job is swapped.

createdVia.description: If known, how this job was created, e.g. from a scheduled job or the WebPortal

workflow.description: The path of the workflow to which this jobs belongs.

workflowId.description: The id of the underlying workflow.

state.description: The current state of the job.

nodeMessages.description: A collection with all node messages that occurred during execution.

example.message: Execute failed: Cannot connect to server.

isOutdated.description: If the underlying workflow has been updated, i.e. the job doesn't reflect the current workflow.

createdAt.description: The date and time this job was created.

hasReport.description: Whether the job is able to create a report.

isSwapped.description: Indicates if the job is persisted to the object store rather than loaded in an executor.

outputResources.description: The output resources that can be accessed via the respective REST endpoint after execution finished successfully.

finishedExecutionAt.description: The date and time this job finished execution.

startedExecutionAt.description: The date and time this job started execution.

inputResources.description: The input resources that were used to execute this job.

executionContext.description: The execution context this job is bound to.

discardAfterSuccessfulExec.description: If the job should be discarded after a successful execution.

discardAfterFailedExec.description: If the job should be discarded after an unsuccessful execution.

actions.description: The job's actions that are triggered after execution.

example.message: Execute failed: Cannot connect to server.

NodeMsg.description: A node message that occurred during execution.

onSuccess.description: If the mail should be sent after a successful execution.

onFailure.description: If the mail should be sent after an unsuccessful execution.

onInactivityDiscard.description: If the mail should be sent after a job has been discarded due to inactivity.

appendNodeMessages.description: If the mail should contain the node messages.

reportFormats.description: The format of the report that shall be attached to the mail in case the workflow has a report; if empty no report will be generated.

SaveReportAction.title: SaveReport
SaveReportAction.description: Action for saving reports on the server.

location.description: The location on the hub where the report shall be stored.

appendTimeStamp.description: If the timestamp shall be appended to the report; if set to false previous reports with the given name and location are overwritten.

workflowUri.description: The ID or path of the workflow for which a job should be created.

onSuccess.description: If the workflow should be called after a successful execution.

onFailure.description: If the workflow should be called after an unsuccessful execution.

reset.description: If the workflow should be reset before execution.

actions.description: The actions passed to the created job.

discardAfterSuccessfulExec.description: If the resulting job should be discarded after a successful execution.

discardAfterFailedExec.description: If the resulting job should be discarded after a failed execution.

WorkflowConfiguration.description: The configuration that is applied when the job gets loaded. This is dependent on the configuration nodes used in the workflow. A template of the configuration can be accessed via a GET request to the configuration endpoint of the workflow _/repository/*workflowId:configuration_.

KNIME-Job-Id.description: The ID of the job that was created as part of the request.
